mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days whenever FEDFUNDS remains above its 12-month trailing average.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following high-FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly lower than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor conditions that foreshadow weaker consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where the trigger fires, forward 20d SPX returns are positive, or the effect size is not statistically distinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST reflects broad-based consumer and housing demand strength that jointly signals durable economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; neither series alone carries this signal because retail strength without housing follow-through often reflects transitory spending.
failure_condition: Joint-positive RSAFS/HOUST periods produce 60d forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO reflects overheating industrial capacity utilization that raises input cost pressure risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in margin compression risk; this relationship is expected to reverse in early-cycle recoveries where INDPRO acceleration signals healthy restocking.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration is associated with positive or neutral 5d forward returns in late-cycle periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05), contradicting the hypothesized negative sign.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, monetary policy enters a restrictive regime in which the cumulative drag on credit availability and corporate investment outweighs near-term momentum, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days that intensify the longer the rate remains elevated.
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns during FEDFUNDS > 4.5% periods are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or hit rate for negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Weakening PAYEMS growth combined with a simultaneous rise in UNRATE constitutes a confirmed labor market inflection that is more predictive of recessionary equity drawdowns than either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when both conditions co-occur.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 0 AND UNRATE 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce forward 20d returns not significantly different from baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction term fails to improve Rank IC over either single-series model by more than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending, which supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days on a persistent basis regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward 5d returns stays below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods do not outperform negative growth periods with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while INDPRO growth simultaneously stalls or declines, the economy enters a stagflation-like regime in which margin compression and monetary tightening risk dominate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this joint pattern is distinct from either high inflation alone or weak production alone.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change > 3% AND INDPRO 3-month change <= 0
failure_condition: During joint-trigger stagflation periods, 60d forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or hit rate for negative returns falls below 55% across at least two distinct historical episodes.
